column1: Hello Debby. The National Hurricane Center has upgraded Debby to a Tropical Storm. No real changes to the track, but they do bump up winds 5 mph upon landfall which would upgrade Debby to a hurricane. They also slow it down as it approaches land not making landfall until Monday afternoon.

If anyone lives in the big bend area where this is forecasted to hit, I'd be expecting a major hurricane before landfall... there's 36 hrs between now and landfall and that's nothing but bath water in the gulf. I'd be leaving town for a few days or boarding up windows
true you just never know what these storms will truly do until the last minute . They have been known to make drastic changes .
I totally agree. I've been in Florida for 40 years and have seen it all.
I’ve been here 25 . Some of the tropical storms can do extensive damage just with the rainfall .
True, especially in flood-prone areas. South Florida has been underwater several times this year.

yep . Our house was one that flooded n we are possibly facing again . Our house is raised but we have a separate living space that is not and many homes in the neighborhood that are not and they all flooded and redid their homes . I’m praying for everyone that it doesn’t happen again. We are on the Weeki Wachee River .
you must be new here

What kind of chances are we looking at for this thing to intensify beyond a Cat 2 or 3?
PCB here trying to make plans accordingly
none. It's already a tropical storm. ( with lots of rain.)
none.
What would the timing window for tornado impact be for the Tampa, and south of Tampa areas, be?
all day tomorrow, Sunday 🙂
it's in the gulf
exactly. Which means tornado activity will be east of it. I’m just asking what the timing of that potential will be for this area.

sigh. I asked a legit question because the local news has said there will be tornados. We have had them in the past. My own home was affected by one. I’m not a newbie. Just asking him about time frame of a known concern.
you do realize we commonly get tornadoes in tropical weather right ??
Denis Phillips has actually said the northeast quadrant is the most likely area to get tornados in this storm. 😊
right. And that’s where we are at right now. Trying to figure out the timing of the worst of it before I medicate my kitties to keep them calm in case anything happens.

yes, I was just explaining for anyone who might think we wouldn't get any tornadoes. 😊
I hope your kitties are ok. 💖

thank you. I’m going to hold off until tomorrow unless it picks up tonight. The night we got 12” of rain one of my girls had a seizure from the stress. Apparently it’s a common thing, so now we try to avoid it. 🙂
alrighty guess you live in a bubble …. Google how many actually touchdown a year in pinellas county
Was able to cut my lawn earlier today before the rains came. Have all the necessities to live after a storm hits. Stay safe !

I remember the last Tropical Storm "Debby" in 2012. On Sunday, June 24 at 3pm, I just got off the air at the Q-105 studios in St. Petersburg when some of the worst was just beginning. Heading north over the Howard Frankland Bridge was wild... huge waves crashing over the wall and across the highway, and probably the hardest rain in which I've ever driven. The entire trip home to Pasco was a crazy ride!
a lot of flooding with that one-I remember it well
This is the one we drove down through!
yes, that’s when we moved to the Sunshine State😂
it's wild when the waves crash over the sides! I moved to the middle lane so fast lol

I would say as the map looks right now we will be experiencing rain bands depending on how close it comes to the shore line. i would say a significant amount of rain until Monday. We thought this thing would be fast moving predicting it between tonight and tomorrow morning but now Sunday night into Monday .... annoying I say but gets you more time to prepare but I don't think it's gonna be a big event in our area... I'm in Pasco too... but if your area tends to flood with heavy rain I would predict that be the case... mild to moderate amount of flooding.

Slower forward speed = farther south it crosses the state?

yes and no. A slower travel could add to intensity which could make it want to lean in, but high pressure and upper level steering currents will have the final say. It's already moving north, no real steering currents that should draw it east at an earlier point. My concern is that the intensity forecast is a bit too conservative at this point, but I have high confidence in the track so far. Just a hobbiest in weather, so it's just my 2 cents.
thank you

you're welcome. I pray I'm correct, due to the models and weather folks I've found trust in, and don't want anything unexpected to happen to folks in impact areas. I'm in one myself, in an RV, so I've been glued to forecast info. That said, opinions and models are constantly changing, so hope for the best, prepare for the weird.

Irma came from Africa and was a hurricane way before she hit

wow really!? It wasn’t even a storm yet? They were giving us information on what the models could give us based on a “cluster” of storms. It wasn’t even a closed low or anything. Sheesh! 🙄 hence the “windshield wiper affect” with the models.

until our technology is better, we need to let these storms play out. We need to always be prepared. My point is that constantly focusing on a “potential “ storm for days, if not a couple of weeks, simply causes stress for everyone. We need better technology. Being in a 24/7 cycle with updates (with everything!) can be daunting. Preparation is key.
totally agree, but no need to stress though if you prepare and just listen to Denis Phillips and rule #7. Saying the professionals totally missed this one is wrong.
I stand corrected on my comment. I’m a weather “nut”. I wanted to be a meteorologist but I stink at math and science. 😂 I’m just fascinated. Thank you for holding me accountable.

For those of you that use Withlacoochee electric company for your power source, they have a wonderful generator program. They'll actually put the payments onto your bill each month so you don't have to have all the money up front. It's a lifesaver for those of you who are worried about power outages during storms or hurricanes. You can find out more information on their website.

Thank you. I had looked into that 1-2 years ago but it wasn't available. I'll have to check it out again. Although they're really fast about getting the power back on. I only lost power for 2 days during Irma. I am grateful to have them as my power company, their rates are not crazy high.

I took advantage of the program myself. It's wonderful security knowing that you have a generator attached to your home that flips on automatically in the event of an outage. It gives you real peace of mind. And they do all the work through Withlacoochee. They cleaned up everything and left my yard in beautiful condition. I had the propane tank buried as well. It was well worth the money, and it is also something that adds value to your home if you were to ever sell it. Comes with an 8 or 10 year warranty depending on whether you go with an 8 or 10 year payment plan. At the time when I got mine, it was a zero interest program. You needed to put down a down payment in order to start the process once you qualified. They did ask that you had a certain credit score so be aware of that. But they can answer all your questions by getting a hold of them via phone.
Thank you for sharing that info. I'm sure it is peace of mind. I will look further into it. 🙂
